tests.details: init.sh --non-interactive completed; project tree present, verify-install reported 52 passed checks

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh reports Context7 MCP installed and configured

tests.name: no_residual_backup_dir
tests.details: .claude-backup absent (init log shows it was removed automatically)

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: Running init.sh from $FRAMEWORK exits 1 with 'Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo' (PR #18 confirmed fixed)

tests.name: baseline_phase2_built
tests.details: Manually advanced phase-state.current_phase to 2 (no auto-advance script — known U-D); then ran process-checklist start-feature, all 6 build_loop steps, test-gate.sh --record-feature; feature 'uat-feat-1' present in .claude/build-progress.json features_completed

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script auto-advances .current_phase after phase gate
framework_bugs.reproduction: After init at sponsored_poc/web/standard, no documented script writes phase-state.current_phase from 0 to 1/2. Required manual jq edit to proceed to Phase 2 baseline.
framework_bugs.expected: A scripted path (e.g., process-checklist or check-phase-gate) advances .current_phase after each gate's checklist completes
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase remained at 0 after init; had to set it manually with jq to enable build_loop usage

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: upgrade-project.sh --to-production exits with code 1. The user-visible 'Upgrade Complete' banner prints, all phase-state changes commit successfully, but the script's tail block 'Refreshing framework helper scripts (BL-009, BL-015)' runs cp src dst where SCRIPT_DIR resolves to the project's own scripts/ directory (because the project copy of upgrade-project.sh was invoked). cp 'are identical (not copied)' returns non-zero and set -euo pipefail aborts the script after Upgrade Complete. Reproduces 100% on a fresh project.

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: .features_completed in build-progress.json still contains uat-feat-1; build_loop reset to clean state (post-feature_recorded) which is expected

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Upgrade output includes governance review pointers, CLAUDE.md POC-watermark cleanup notes, PROJECT_INTAKE.md governance section pointer

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: Pre-staged .claude/pending-approval.json caused upgrade-project.sh --to-production to exit 1 with '[FAIL] upgrade blocked — pending user decision' and pointer to scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve/--clear (PR #17 / U-F fix confirmed working)

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh header contains both BL-006 (2026-04-24) and BL-015 (2026-04-25) changelog entries

tests.name: no_template_placeholders_in_output
tests.details: grep of upgrade output for __PROJECT_NAME__/__PLATFORM__/__LANGUAGE__/__TRACK__ returns 0 hits

tests.name: poc_mode_lookup_succeeded
tests.details: Upgrade detected sponsored_poc → production transition (PR #17 / U-E fix confirmed); no 'project is not in POC mode' false negative on first run

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh --to-production exits 1 due to self-cp at end of script
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) init.sh non-interactive sponsored_poc/web/standard. 2) jq set current_phase=2. 3) yes | bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production; echo "EXIT=$?". Observed EXIT=1 on a clean repro tempdir.
framework_bugs.expected: Upgrade exits 0 after printing 'Upgrade Complete' (state changes are persisted; should signal success)
framework_bugs.actual: After 'Upgrade Complete' banner, the 'Refreshing framework helper scripts (BL-009, BL-015)' step at scripts/upgrade-project.sh:1469-1480 runs `cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/$helper" "scripts/$helper"`. When the project's copy of upgrade-project.sh is invoked (the canonical pattern), $SCRIPT_DIR resolves to the project's own scripts/ directory, so cp source and dest are the same file. macOS cp emits 'are identical (not copied)' and returns non-zero; set -euo pipefail aborts. The state mutations (phase-state poc_mode removal, last_upgrade stamp, chore(upgrade) commit) DO succeed because they happen earlier, so the user sees a successful state change but a failed script return code. Fix candidates: guard the cp with `[ "$SCRIPT_DIR/$helper" -ef "scripts/$helper" ] || cp ...` OR use cp from $ORCHESTRATOR_ROOT/scripts/$helper instead of $SCRIPT_DIR/$helper, OR `cp -f` with explicit success when src=dst. Same exit-1 reproduces at the upstream framework level too (any upgrade run from the project).

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: grep for password|api_key|secret|token in committed project files returned only template/doc references (release.yml comments about Vercel/secrets, evaluation-prompts security audit checklist mentioning secrets-management concepts). No real credentials present.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: .claude/process-audit.log absent — no FORCE/reset events occurred (N/A per runbook)

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD matches starting SHA d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494; status shows only pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ entries (sweep artifacts, not from this agent)

summary: Init non-interactive and Section A all pass; upgrade reaches 'Upgrade Complete' and persists state (phase preserved, features preserved, BL-015 sentinel respected, no placeholders, U-E poc_mode lookup works) but the trailing helper-refresh self-cp causes upgrade-project.sh --to-production to exit 1 — Critical regression in PR #17 cleanup logic.
